features.name: Industry-Wide Reporting & Analytics Gap — DFVA as Independent Analytics Standard
features.detail: Confirmed across 3 competitors (CourseLeaf, CourseLoop, Coursedog): no curriculum platform delivers adequate reporting/analytics. ListEdTech: 'These are data questions, not workflow questions.' DFVA's scoring dashboards fill the gap as an independent analytics layer.

features.name: Lightcast Data Freshness — Real-Time LMI Alternative
features.detail: Gartner reviews confirm Lightcast data is 'years behind' and 'could be outdated.' Combined with high pricing, this creates an opening for DFVA to use fresher, supplementary data sources (real-time job postings, AI-synthesized demand signals).

features.name: Market Convergence Validation — DFVA Thesis Confirmed by Third Independent Source
features.detail: ListEdTech, RFP.wiki, and InnoTechToday all confirm the market is converging toward data-centric 'academic operations platforms.' No vendor owns the analytics layer. DFVA's independent scoring standard has a validated market window — but it's closing as Coursedog ships LMI.

features.name: Structured Curriculum Data as Market Differentiator — Incumbents Store Degrees as Unstructured HTML
features.detail: GitHub coursedog-importer project (real university migration, 9 PRs Mar-Apr 2026) confirms both Coursedog and Modern Campus store degree requirements as freeform HTML blocks — NOT structured data. No curriculum platform in the market supports programmatic analysis of degree requirements. DFVA's structured assessment model is a fundamentally different data architecture, not just better analytics.

features.name: API Maturity as Competitive Differentiator — Developer Experience Layer
features.detail: Coursedog API docs are inaccurate (password field naming), dual-ID system confuses developers, undocumented required parameters (doIntegration=true), and non-standard response formats. Developers must inspect browser network tabs to use the API. Clean, well-documented DFVA API would contrast sharply. Phase 1 built: API key management backend (19 tests), developer portal landing page, competitive comparison matrix, code snippet library, NavBar integration.

features.name: Second Confirmed Coursedog Migration — Universal Data Extraction Pain Pattern
features.detail: Mercy University 25Live → Coursedog migration (June 2026) is the SECOND independent project requiring custom Selenium/Python extraction code because legacy systems provide no structured data export. Combined with the first migration (Modern Campus → Coursedog, Mar-Apr 2026), a pattern is confirmed: every Coursedog migration requires custom scraping. DFVA should position as the migration-safe alternative — structured data from day one, no HTML scraping required.

features.name: TechnologyOne Reporting Gap — DFVA as T1's Missing Analytics Layer
features.detail: GitHub technologyone-analyser project (active through June 2026, 10+ issues) is a complete web app built because T1's native reporting is broken. Parses proprietary T1 formats (.t1etlp, .t1dm, .t1db, .t1xl). Requires air-gap security because T1 data is too sensitive for cloud. This directly confirms the analytics gap for DFVA's Go8 target competitor. DFVA can position as the analytics standard T1 itself cannot deliver.

features.name: Cross-Platform Scraper Regression Storm — HTML Fragility as DFVA Differentiator
features.detail: On July 5, 2026, the cc-coursemap production scraper suffered a 6-state simultaneous regression (MN, MA, CT, VT, WV, PA) across Coursedog/CourseLeaf. A single upstream HTML change broke all parsers. Combined with the open 'Prod health check failing' incident (June 23), this confirms HTML-based curriculum data is becoming MORE fragile, not less. Fourth independent confirmation of the HTML data problem. Every cc-coursemap regression is a case study for DFVA's structured, API-first, no-scraping approach.
